_meta.description: sCure central Error Registry — the single source of truth for every error/warning. Each record has a stable, unique numeric code whose FIRST DIGIT identifies the SRS section/screen the error is documented in (3=Setup, 4=Setup Name, 5=Connect Organization, 6=Home, 7=Cure Process, 8=Cure History, 9=Error Management: device/hardware alerts + cross-cutting system faults). The code is carried UNCHANGED across every layer (C++ driver/daemon -> Flask backend -> React UI); the originating subsystem is recorded in the per-record "layer" field. CS may edit message, description, troubleshooting and supportUrl; code, key, severity, category and layer are system-defined.

ranges.label: Home Screen (material list, recipe builder, CSV import, view-all, start cure)
Error Management — device/hardware alerts (9001-9079) + cross-cutting system/backend faults (9080-9099)

errors.description: During setup the serial number could not be restored from the SD backup to Storage. Provisioning cannot continue until a valid serial number is available.
troubleshooting: Check the SD card is seated and readable
Verify the backup configuration file exists on SD
Reboot and retry setup
Contact support to re-provision the serial number

errors.description: The device could not obtain a serial number from the cloud within the allowed number of attempts during setup (problem communicating with the cloud).
troubleshooting: Check the internet connection
Verify the device can reach the sCure cloud service
Retry the request
Contact support if the cloud remains unreachable

errors.description: During setup the device has no internet connection, so it cannot request a serial number from the cloud. The user can connect the network and retry.
troubleshooting: Connect the ethernet cable
Verify the network/router is online
Retry once the connection is restored

errors.description: The device name entered on the Setup Name screen is empty. A non-empty name is required to continue.
troubleshooting: Enter a name for the device
Avoid leading/trailing spaces only

errors.description: The backend could not persist a data file (user materials or print history) to the device storage.
troubleshooting: Check available space on the device
Reboot the system and retry
Contact support if the fault persists

errors.description: The configuration was saved to primary Storage but the secondary backup copy to the SD card failed. Whether this blocks the flow or is a warning-only follows system policy (SRS OI-6).
troubleshooting: Check the SD card is inserted and writable
Verify free space on the SD card
Reboot and retry; the primary configuration is already saved

errors.description: The selected organization CSV file does not contain a valid organization ID (UUID).
troubleshooting: Select the correct organization CSV exported from the portal
Verify the file contains a valid organization UUID

errors.description: The recipe builder cannot save a program without a name.
troubleshooting: Enter a program name before saving

errors.description: The recipe builder cannot save a program with zero steps.
troubleshooting: Add at least one step to the program

errors.description: An N₂ purge step is not followed by the required sequence: N₂ → (Cure | Bleaching) → Cooling.
troubleshooting: After an N₂ purge, add a Cure or Bleaching step
After that Cure/Bleaching, add a Cooling step

errors.description: A non-cooling step's temperature is lower than the previous non-cooling step. Temperatures must not decrease except in a Cooling step.
troubleshooting: Raise the step temperature to at least the previous step's temperature
Use a Cooling step to lower the temperature

errors.description: A Cooling step's target temperature is not lower than the temperature of the preceding step.
troubleshooting: Set the Cooling target below the previous step's temperature

errors.description: An imported program CSV is missing a required header or contains no data row.
troubleshooting: Use a CSV exported by the recipe builder
Check the header row and that at least one step is present

errors.description: A row in the imported CSV names a process that is not recognised (expected Heating, Drying, Cure, Bleaching, Cooling or Nitrogen).
troubleshooting: Correct the process name in the offending row
Re-export the CSV from the recipe builder

errors.description: A row in the imported CSV has a temperature outside the accepted 20–80°C range.
troubleshooting: Set the row temperature between 20°C and 80°C

errors.description: A row in the imported CSV has a time outside the accepted range (1–120, Bleaching 1–720). Note: per-step time has since been removed from the recipe model (SRS OI-17); this check is retained only for legacy CSV files.
troubleshooting: Correct the time value in the offending row
Re-export the CSV from the current recipe builder

errors.description: The imported CSV defines more than the maximum of 2 nitrogen purge steps.
troubleshooting: Reduce the program to at most 2 nitrogen purge steps

errors.description: The recipe builder could not save the generated program CSV to USB; it falls back to a browser download.
troubleshooting: Insert a writable USB stick and retry
Use the downloaded file from the browser instead

errors.description: A Cure Together batch may only combine prints of the same material. Two or more different materials were selected.
troubleshooting: Select prints that all use the same material
Run different materials as separate cures

errors.description: Start Cure was pressed with no material or print selected.
troubleshooting: Select a material or a recent print before starting a cure

errors.description: The selected program cannot run as configured — e.g. it is nitrogen-only while nitrogen is disabled, so every step would be skipped.
troubleshooting: Enable nitrogen in Settings, or
Choose a program that has non-nitrogen steps

errors.description: The pre-cure hardware check did not pass (e.g. chamber not ready, door/seal open or N₂ line not pressurised). The cure was not started and no outputs were energised.
troubleshooting: Close and seal the chamber door
Verify the N₂ line pressure (if nitrogen is enabled)
Wait for the chamber to report ready, then retry
Run diagnostics from Settings if the check keeps failing

errors.description: A cure operation requested a target temperature outside the accepted 20–80°C range. The backend rejected the command and energised no outputs.
troubleshooting: Edit the recipe step so the target is between 20°C and 80°C
Re-import the program CSV if the value came from a file

errors.description: A Cure (405 nm) or Bleaching (450 nm) step requested a UV intensity outside the accepted 5–100% range. The backend rejected the command.
troubleshooting: Set the UV intensity between 5% and 100%
Re-import the program CSV if the value came from a file

errors.description: A cooling step requested a mode other than fast, medium or slow. The backend rejected the command.
troubleshooting: Set the cooling mode to fast, medium or slow
Re-import the program CSV if the value came from a file

errors.description: During a cure the chamber failed to move toward its target across five consecutive 5-minute stall checks. The user is warned that continuing may cause distortions, and may Continue or Abort.
troubleshooting: Choose Abort to stop, or Continue at your own risk
Check heater/cooling operation and chamber insulation
Run diagnostics from Settings

errors.description: A cure run was aborted (by the user from the active phase, or from the Temperature Warning). The session is logged with status aborted and the device returns to Home.
troubleshooting: Review the cure history entry for the aborted run
Restart the cure when ready

errors.description: The network diagnostics endpoint was called with an unsupported tool name (expected ping, traceroute or nslookup).
troubleshooting: Select one of the supported diagnostic tools
Retry the diagnostic from the Network screen

errors.description: An export operation could not locate a mounted USB drive on the device.
troubleshooting: Insert a USB stick into the machine
Wait a few seconds for it to mount, then retry
Try a different USB port or stick

errors.description: The backend found a USB drive but failed to write the file to it (full, read-only or removed mid-write).
troubleshooting: Check the USB stick has free space and is not write-protected
Do not remove the USB stick during export
Reformat the USB stick (FAT32) and retry

errors.description: The backend failed to copy the system logs to the USB drive.
troubleshooting: Insert a writable USB stick and retry
Check available space on the USB drive

errors.description: The backend could not read the device network status (IP / MAC / gateway).
troubleshooting: Check the ethernet cable and link LEDs
Reboot the system
Verify network settings

errors.description: A network diagnostic command (ping / traceroute / nslookup) failed to execute on the device.
troubleshooting: Verify the target address is reachable
Check the network connection and retry

errors.description: The backend update process failed while installing a software package from USB.
troubleshooting: Re-download the update package and copy it to USB
Verify the package version and integrity
Retry the update from Settings → Update Software

errors.description: No update package (or its manifest) could be found on the connected USB drive.
troubleshooting: Copy the .scu update package and its manifest to the USB root
Insert the USB stick and retry

errors.description: The update package failed SHA-256 integrity verification (missing/malformed checksum or a mismatch — corrupt or tampered package). Installation is blocked (EN 18031 secure update).
troubleshooting: Re-download the update package from the official portal
Verify the checksum/manifest matches the package
Do not install packages from untrusted sources

errors.description: The UI could not reach the local hardware API (Flask backend). Hardware commands cannot be sent.
troubleshooting: Reboot the system
Verify the sCure service is running
Contact support if the fault persists
